title: Enforce the seven gates (P1-P7) via the CLI

acceptanceCriteria.text: All gate tests pass (P1/P4/P5/P6/P7) via node --test

title: feat: chalk decisions

acceptanceCriteria.text: add a test in `test/protocol.test.mjs` asserting it shows a decision after `chalk decision`

issue.body:
- [ ] `chalk decisions` prints the contents of .chalk/decisions.md
- [ ] add a test in `test/protocol.test.mjs` asserting it shows a decision after `chalk decision`

findings.note: `decisions()` calls readFileSync(s.p.decisions) without a guard; if .chalk/decisions.md were missing it would throw an unfriendly error. In practice init always creates the file, so both criteria are satisfied.

Test only covers the happy path (decision exists). No assertion of clean behavior when the log is empty/just-initialized, but the criterion only requires showing a decision after `chalk decision`, which is covered.

plan:
## Plan

**Approach:** Add a tiny read-only `decisions` command to the `cmds` map in `bin/chalk.mjs` that prints `.chalk/decisions.md`. Mirror the existing `decision`/`lesson` pattern (`Store.open()` ‚Üí `s.p.decisions`); `readFileSync` is already imported. Simplest, reuses existing store paths ‚Äî no new utilities.

**Steps:**
1. In `bin/chalk.mjs`, after the `decision({...})` command (~line 875), add `decisions()` that does `const s = Store.open();` and `console.log(readFileSync(s.p.decisions, 'utf8'))` (guard with `existsSync` or fall back to empty ‚Äî but `init` always creates the file, so a direct read is fine).
2. Add a one-line entry in `printHelp()` near the `chalk decision` line (~1011) for discoverability.

**Files:**
- `bin/chalk.mjs` ‚Äî new `decisions` cmd in the `cmds` object (reuse `Store.open()` + `s.p.decisions` from `lib/store.mjs`; `readFileSync` already imported); help text.
- `test/protocol.test.mjs` ‚Äî new test.

**Test:** Add `test('decisions ‚Äî prints logged decisions', ...)`: `chalk(d,'init','--name','demo')`; `chalk(d,'decision','use sqlite for the ledger','--why','simplicity')`; then `const r = chalk(d,'decisions')`; assert `r.code===0` and `assert.match(r.out, /use sqlite for the ledger/)`. Fails today (unknown command exits 1), passes with the new command.

title: feat: chalk lesson list

acceptanceCriteria.text: `chalk lesson list` prints the recorded lessons from .chalk/lessons.md (the same memory injected into agents)
add a test in `test/protocol.test.mjs` asserting `lesson list` shows a previously-added lesson

issue.body:
- [ ] `chalk lesson list` prints the recorded lessons from .chalk/lessons.md (the same memory injected into agents)
- [ ] `chalk lesson \"...\"` (adding) still works unchanged
- [ ] add a test in `test/protocol.test.mjs` asserting `lesson list` shows a previously-added lesson

findings.note: `chalk lesson list` reads s.lessons(1000) while context injection uses s.lessons(15); criterion 1 says 'the same memory injected into agents'. They share the source file but the cap differs, so list could surface older lessons not actually injected. Harmless/arguably more complete, not blocking.

Dispatch treats `_[0] === 'list'` as the list subcommand, so a literal lesson text starting with the word 'list' (e.g. `chalk lesson list the deps`) would be misrouted. Edge case, acceptable for the contract.
The added test only checks the happy path (one added lesson appears). No test for the empty-state ('no lessons recorded yet.') branch, but criteria 1-3 are all directly exercised.

plan:
## Approach
Add a `list` subcommand to the existing `lesson` handler in `bin/chalk.mjs`: if `_[0] === 'list'`, print recorded lessons via the existing `Store.lessons()`; otherwise keep the current add behavior unchanged. Reuses the store reader that already feeds agent context ‚Äî minimal, no new module.

## Steps
1. In `bin/chalk.mjs` `lesson({ _, flags })`, branch at top: `if (_[0] === 'list')` ‚Üí open store, get `s.lessons(1000)`, print each line (and a friendly note if empty), then `return`.
2. Leave the existing add path (`_.join(' ') || flags.text` ‚Üí `appendLesson`) untouched so `chalk lesson "..."` still works.
3. Add usage line for `chalk lesson list` in the help block (~line 1019).

## Files
- `bin/chalk.mjs` ‚Äî `lesson()` handler (~line 885) + help text. Reuse `Store.open()` and `s.lessons()` from `lib/store.mjs`.
- `test/protocol.test.mjs` ‚Äî new test.

## Test
Add to `test/protocol.test.mjs`: `init`, `chalk(d,'lesson','prefer reusing existing utilities')` (code 0), then `chalk(d,'lesson','list')` ‚Äî assert `.code === 0` and `.out` matches `/prefer reusing existing utilities/`. Fails today because `lesson list` currently treats "list" as lesson text and never prints stored lessons.

title: feat: chalk log --grep <text>

acceptanceCriteria.text: `chalk log --grep <text>` shows only update events whose title contains <text> (case-insensitive), still honoring `--n`, `--type`, and `--reverse`
add a test in `test/protocol.test.mjs` asserting `--grep` filters AND that it composes with another flag (e.g. `--type`)

issue.body:
- [ ] `chalk log --grep <text>` shows only update events whose title contains <text> (case-insensitive), still honoring `--n`, `--type`, and `--reverse`
- [ ] without `--grep`, output is unchanged
- [ ] add a test in `test/protocol.test.mjs` asserting `--grep` filters AND that it composes with another flag (e.g. `--type`)

findings.note: The new test composes --grep with --type and verifies case-insensitivity + plain-unchanged, satisfying criterion 3. It does not assert --grep composes with --n or --reverse, but criterion 1's --n/--reverse behavior is structurally guaranteed (grep filters before slice(-n)/reverse, identical to the already-tested --type path).
String(u.title) coerces missing titles to 'undefined' which could spuriously match a grep for 'undefined', but update titles are always required in practice, so no realistic impact.

plan:
## Plan

**Approach:** Mirror the existing `--type` filter in `cmds.log` (bin/chalk.mjs:921) ‚Äî add a `--grep` filter on `u.title` applied to the full set *before* `.slice(-n)`, so `--n`/`--reverse`/`--json` continue to compose unchanged. Reusing the same filter slot is the smallest, pattern-consistent change.

**Steps:**
1. In `cmds.log`, after the `flags.type` filter line (925), add: `if (flags.grep) all = all.filter((u) => String(u.title).toLowerCase().includes(String(flags.grep).toLowerCase()));`
2. Leave the rest (slice, reverse, json/plain output) untouched ‚Äî `--grep` automatically honors `--n`, `--type`, `--reverse`, `--json`.
3. Add the `--grep` token to the help line at bin/chalk.mjs:1028.

**Files:**
- `bin/chalk.mjs` ‚Äî `cmds.log` (line ~925) + help text (line 1028). Reuse the existing `all.filter` chain pattern.
- `test/protocol.test.mjs` ‚Äî new test (append after the `--type` test ~line 117).

**Test:** Add `test('log --grep ‚Äî filters by title substring (case-insensitive), composes with --type; plain unchanged', ‚Ä¶)`:
- seed: `update 'deploy api'`, `update 'fix Login bug'`, `update 'deploy worker' --type milestone-hit`.
- `log --grep deploy` includes both deploy lines, excludes `fix Login bug`.
- case-insensitivity: `log --grep LOGIN` includes `fix Login bug`.
- composition: `log --grep deploy --type milestone-hit` shows only `deploy worker`, not `deploy api`.
- `log` (no `--grep`) still shows all three.

This fails today (the `grep` flag is ignored, so `--grep deploy` would show all events including `fix Login bug`) and passes with the filter added.

title: fix: chalk lesson dispatch misroutes lesson text beginning with 'list'

acceptanceCriteria.text: Only the bare `chalk lesson list` (no trailing text) triggers the list subcommand
Recording a lesson whose text starts with the word 'list' records it correctly
A test covers a lesson text beginning with 'list'

issue.body:
`lesson()` treats `_[0] === 'list'` as the list subcommand, so `chalk lesson "list the deps first"` (or `chalk lesson list the deps`) is misrouted to the listing path instead of recording the lesson.

- [ ] Only the bare `chalk lesson list` (no trailing text) triggers the list subcommand
- [ ] Recording a lesson whose text starts with the word 'list' records it correctly
- [ ] A test covers a lesson text beginning with 'list'

_filed by `chalk retro` (self-healing)_

findings.note: The single-word lesson `chalk lesson list` (text being exactly 'list') remains indistinguishable from the bare list subcommand and so cannot be recorded. This is an inherent ambiguity and consistent with criterion 1 (only bare `chalk lesson list` triggers the list subcommand); multi-word texts beginning with 'list' record correctly via the `_.length === 1` guard.

Tests cover multi-word 'list ...' recording (line 50) and bare 'list' listing (line 41), satisfying all criteria. A negative-assertion test confirming the 'list ...' text does NOT print existing lessons would harden coverage, but the file-content assertion is sufficient.

plan:
## Plan

**Approach:** One-line fix in the `lesson` command dispatch in `bin/chalk.mjs`. The bug: `if (_[0] === 'list')` routes to the list subcommand whenever the first positional token is `list`, so an unquoted lesson like `chalk lesson list comprehensions are great` is swallowed. Gate it on `_.length === 1` so only the bare `chalk lesson list` lists.

**Steps:**
1. In `lesson({ _, flags })`, change the guard from `if (_[0] === 'list')` to `if (_[0] === 'list' && _.length === 1)`.
2. Everything below (text join + `appendLesson`) already handles the record path; multi-token `list ...` now falls through and records correctly.

**Files:**
- `bin/chalk.mjs` ‚Äî `lesson()` command (~line 887). No new utils; reuses `s.appendLesson` / `s.lessons`.
- `test/protocol.test.mjs` ‚Äî add a test (mirror the existing `lesson list` test at line 41 for setup helpers `scratch`/`chalk`).

**Test:** Add `test('lesson ‚Äî text starting with "list" is recorded, not routed to list subcommand')`: `init`, then `chalk(d, 'lesson', 'list', 'comprehensions', 'are', 'great')` exits 0, and assert `.chalk/lessons.md` includes `list comprehensions are great`. This FAILS today (text is misrouted to the list printer, nothing recorded) and passes after the guard change. The existing bare-`lesson list` test (line 41) still guards regression.

title: fix: chalk lesson list cap diverges from the memory injected into agents

acceptanceCriteria.text: `chalk lesson list` and `buildContext` use the same cap so the listed lessons match what is injected
If a fuller view is intentional, document the distinction and/or gate it behind an explicit flag (e.g. `--all`)
A test asserts the listed lessons correspond to the injected set

issue.body:
`chalk lesson list` reads `s.lessons(1000)` while context injection uses the default `s.lessons(15)`. Criterion 1 promises 'the same memory injected into agents', but the differing cap means `list` can surface older lessons that are never actually injected, misrepresenting what the planner/executor see.

- [ ] `chalk lesson list` and `buildContext` use the same cap so the listed lessons match what is injected
- [ ] If a fuller view is intentional, document the distinction and/or gate it behind an explicit flag (e.g. `--all`)
- [ ] A test asserts the listed lessons correspond to the injected set

_filed by `chalk retro` (self-healing)_

findings.note: The correspondence test relies on regex filtering of 'lesson number N' lines from both outputs; it confirms the default list equals the injected set and that --all differs, which adequately covers criteria 1 and 3. It does not assert the exact cap value (15) or that buildContext and lesson list share a literal constant, but both demonstrably route through store.lessons() with the same default LESSON_CAP, so divergence cannot recur without breaking the test.

Criterion 2 is met via the --all flag plus documentation in code comments (store.mjs:20-21) and help text (chalk.mjs:1029); the distinction between listed/injected vs full history is explicitly described.

plan:
## Plan

**Approach:** `chalk lesson list` calls `s.lessons(1000)` while `buildContext` calls `store.lessons()` (default cap **15**) ‚Äî so the list can show lessons that are NOT injected. Fix: make `lesson list` default to the same cap as the injected set, and gate the fuller view behind `--all`. Chosen over silently changing the injected cap, because the 15-cap is the contract agents actually see (criterion 1) and `--all` satisfies the "intentional fuller view" escape hatch (criterion 2). Reuse the existing `Store.lessons(n)` default ‚Äî no new util.

**Steps:**
1. Extract the injected cap into a single shared constant so both paths reference it (e.g. `LESSON_CAP = 15` in `lib/store.mjs`, used as the default of `lessons(n = LESSON_CAP)` and exported).
2. In `bin/chalk.mjs` `lesson()` `list` branch: use `s.lessons()` (the shared default) instead of `s.lessons(1000)`; when `flags.all` is set, pass a large n to show everything.
3. Update help text (`chalk lesson list [--all]`) to document that `list` mirrors the injected set and `--all` shows the full history.

**Files:**
- `lib/store.mjs` ‚Äî `lessons(n)` default + exported `LESSON_CAP`; `buildContext` already uses the default.
- `bin/chalk.mjs` ‚Äî `lesson` command list branch + usage line (~887‚Äì890, ~1027).

**Test:** Add to `test/protocol.test.mjs`: add >15 lessons, then assert `chalk lesson list` output line-count equals the lessons in `chalk context` (the injected set) ‚Äî fails today (list shows all >15, context shows 15) ‚Äî and assert `chalk lesson list --all` shows the older lesson that the default omits.

title: fix: guard chalk decisions against a missing decisions.md

acceptanceCriteria.text: `chalk decisions` prints a clean message (e.g. 'no decisions recorded yet.') when `.chalk/decisions.md` is missing or empty
No unhandled exception/stack trace is shown for the missing-file case
A test covers the missing/empty decisions-log branch

issue.body:
`decisions()` in bin/chalk.mjs calls `readFileSync(s.p.decisions, 'utf8')` with no existence guard. If `.chalk/decisions.md` is absent (e.g. an older or hand-edited store) the command throws a raw ENOENT stack instead of a friendly empty-state message, unlike `lesson list` which handles the empty case.

- [ ] `chalk decisions` prints a clean message (e.g. 'no decisions recorded yet.') when `.chalk/decisions.md` is missing or empty
- [ ] No unhandled exception/stack trace is shown for the missing-file case
- [ ] A test covers the missing/empty decisions-log branch

_filed by `chalk retro` (self-healing)_

findings.note: The added test covers the missing-file case but not a present-but-empty/whitespace-only decisions.md; the code's `.trim()` handles it, yet that path is unexercised. Criterion 3 (missing/empty branch) is still satisfied since both resolve to the same '' code path.

A decisions.md containing only the scaffolded header '# Decisions (ADR-lite)' is non-empty after trim, so it prints the bare header rather than 'no decisions recorded yet.' This is acceptable per the criteria (file not truly empty) but is a mild UX edge case.

plan:
## Plan

**Approach:** Guard the read in the `decisions` command. Two options: (a) inline `existsSync`+empty check in `bin/chalk.mjs`; (b) add a `Store.decisions()` helper mirroring `Store.lessons()`. Pick **(b)** ‚Äî the lessons-learned explicitly say "reuse the Store helper," it keeps the readFileSync guard centralized with `lessons()`/`updates()`, and the command stays a one-liner.

**Steps:**
1. In `lib/store.mjs`, add a `decisions()` method on `Store` (next to `lessons()`/`updates()`): `return existsSync(this.p.decisions) ? readFileSync(this.p.decisions, 'utf8') : '';`
2. In `bin/chalk.mjs` `decisions()` command, replace the raw `readFileSync(s.p.decisions, 'utf8')` with `const text = s.decisions().trim(); console.log(text || 'no decisions recorded yet.');` ‚Äî also treats an empty file as "no decisions."
3. No new imports needed (`existsSync` already imported in store.mjs).

**Files:**
- `lib/store.mjs` ‚Äî add `decisions()` getter (reuse the `existsSync`/`readFileSync` pattern from `lessons()`).
- `bin/chalk.mjs` ‚Äî `decisions()` command handler (~line 878).

**Test:** In `test/protocol.test.mjs`, add `test('decisions ‚Äî clean message when log is missing', ‚Ä¶)`: `chalk init` only (no `chalk decision`), run `chalk decisions`, assert `r.code === 0`, `r.out` matches `/no decisions recorded yet/`, and that no stack trace/`Error` appears in output. This fails today (unhandled `ENOENT` throw, non-zero exit) and passes with the guard.
